name: Jura (Switzerland)
description: A square flag divided vertically: the hoist half white with a red crosier, the fly half red with three white bars.
history: Designed by Paul Boesch in 1943; the crosier recalls the former Prince-Bishopric of Basel and the seven stripes the seven districts of the Bernese Jura. It became the official flag when the canton of Jura was created in 1979.

features.role: red Baselstab (crosier) on the white half
three white bars on red (seven stripes) for the seven districts of the Bernese Jura
